seoTitle: How to Create a Reveal on Scroll Animation in Webflow
metaDescription: Build a smooth scroll-triggered reveal animation in Webflow using the native interactions panel and offset thresholds.
intro: Elements that fade or slide in as users scroll add polish to a page without needing hand-written JavaScript. This guide covers setting up a Scroll Into View trigger in Webflow's interactions panel, configuring the offset percentage, and staggering multiple elements for a sequenced reveal. It's for designers building scroll-based micro-interactions natively.

steps.heading: Open the Interactions panel
steps.body: In the right sidebar, open the Interactions panel where Webflow's scroll and element triggers are configured.

steps.heading: Add a Scroll Into View trigger
steps.body: Select your target element, add a new trigger, and choose Scroll Into View from the trigger type options.

steps.heading: Set the initial hidden state
steps.body: Assign a starting opacity and vertical offset so the element appears hidden and slightly displaced before the animation runs.

steps.heading: Adjust the offset threshold
steps.body: Set a trigger percentage so the animation only starts once the element passes a specific point in the viewport.

steps.heading: Stagger multiple elements
steps.body: Duplicate the trigger rule across sibling elements like cards or headings with a slight delay between each to create a sequenced reveal.

faq.question: Will scroll animations affect page performance?
faq.answer: Webflow's native interactions are hardware-accelerated, so they're generally lightweight compared to custom JavaScript scroll listeners.

faq.question: Can I control how far into the viewport an element must scroll before animating?
faq.answer: Yes, the offset threshold setting lets you delay the trigger until the element crosses a specific percentage of the viewport.

faq.question: Why do my staggered elements all animate at once?
faq.answer: Check that each element has a distinct delay value set in its individual trigger, since identical timings will make them appear simultaneous.

faq.question: Do scroll animations work the same on mobile?
faq.answer: They generally do, but it's worth testing on real mobile devices since scroll behavior and viewport sizing can differ from desktop previews.
